memo: Scope: only the eight excerpts in the packet. Implemented evaluation mechanisms visible in the packet are (a) an execution-feedback loop, where command outputs are fed back as user messages to the Planner (R01-E01) and where a runtime error (an incorrect heater-shaker method name) triggers a documentation lookup and a corrected protocol that ran successfully (R01-E03); (b) a quantitative reactivity signal, normalized advantage values that increase over time and are reused as guidance in iterative optimization (R01-E04); and (c) a human labelling scale used for synthesis-planning assessment, which the source itself describes as inherently subjective and potentially labeler-dependent (R01-E05). Outputs of (a) and (b) feed directly into subsequent planning and experiment selection; the labelling in (c) feeds reported performance assessment. Failure risks supported by the packet: labeler-dependent scoring is an evaluator-bias source that propagates into headline claims (R01-E05); the corrected-protocol episode shows the validator is post-hoc runtime failure, not pre-execution checking, and correction depended on documentation access (R01-E02, R01-E03); the liquid-handler investigation ran without internet access (R01-E06), bounding what the evaluation covers; the cross-coupling setup was not fully automated, with plates moved manually while the source asserts no human decision-making was involved (R01-E07), leaving the human/automation boundary asserted rather than evidenced; and full data, code and prompts are withheld pending US AI regulation (R01-E08), which prevents external verification of any of the above. The packet reports no prior-art/novelty assessment of proposed experiments, no probabilistic calibration of evaluator confidence, no independent replication, and no formal proof checker. Recommendations are limited to what these excerpts support: address the subjective labelling, obtain independent evidence given the release restriction, and clarify the human/automation boundary.

diagnostics.statement: The synthesis-planning assessment relies on a labelling scale the source concedes is subjective and may vary between labelers.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Despite our attempts to better formalize the scale, labelling is inherently subjective and so, may be different between the labelers.

diagnostics.consequence: Reported performance scores may reflect labeler variation rather than system capability, and no inter-labeler agreement measure is reported in the packet to bound that variation.

diagnostics.statement: Validation of generated protocols is post-hoc execution feedback: an incorrect method name was caught only after use, then repaired via documentation lookup, with command outputs routed back to the Planner.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The initial user input along with command outputs are treated as user messages to the Planner.

However, an incorrect heater-shaker module method name was used. Upon making this mistake, Coscientist uses the Docs searcher module to consult the OT-2 documentation. Next, Coscientist modifies the protocol to a corrected version, which ran successfully.

diagnostics.consequence: Errors that do not surface as execution failures, or that occur where documentation is unavailable, may pass uncorrected into physical execution.

diagnostics.statement: Full data, code and prompts are deliberately withheld pending future regulation, while the source claims physical-world experimental capability.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Because of safety concerns, data, code and prompts will be only fully released after the development of US regulations in the field of artificial intelligence and its scientific applications.
Access to documentation enables us to provide sufficient information for Coscientist to conduct experiments in the physical world.

diagnostics.consequence: No third party can reproduce, audit, or replicate the evaluation, so all reported evaluator outputs rest on author-internal evidence.

diagnostics.statement: The human/automation boundary is asserted rather than demonstrated: plates were moved manually while the source claims no human decision-making was involved, and one investigation ran with internet access withheld.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Although our setup is not yet fully automated (plates were moved manually), no human decision-making was involved.
For this investigation, we did not grant access to the internet.

diagnostics.consequence: It is unclear which decisions and information channels are inside the evaluated system, so autonomy claims cannot be scoped or compared across the reported investigations.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Despite our attempts to better formalize the scale, labelling is inherently subjective and so, may be different between the labelers.

success_check.observable: Inter-labeler agreement on the synthesis-planning scale, computed over a common set of items scored by at least two independent labelers
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: A reported agreement statistic with confidence interval is published, and conclusions are re-stated as unchanged only where agreement meets a pre-declared threshold
success_check.evidence_needed: Per-item, per-labeler score tables plus the agreement computation and the pre-declared threshold

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Because of safety concerns, data, code and prompts will be only fully released after the development of US regulations in the field of artificial intelligence and its scientific applications.
The initial user input along with command outputs are treated as user messages to the Planner.

However, an incorrect heater-shaker module method name was used. Upon making this mistake, Coscientist uses the Docs searcher module to consult the OT-2 documentation. Next, Coscientist modifies the protocol to a corrected version, which ran successfully.

success_check.observable: Availability of an audit channel (e.g. gated access for named external reviewers) covering the prompts, command-output logs, and protocol revision traces behind the reported error-and-correction episodes
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: At least one external party outside the author group reports having inspected the traces and states whether the reported corrections are supported
success_check.evidence_needed: Access terms, the released trace artifacts, and the external reviewer's written assessment

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Although our setup is not yet fully automated (plates were moved manually), no human decision-making was involved.
For this investigation, we did not grant access to the internet.

success_check.observable: A per-investigation table enumerating every human action performed, every information source enabled or disabled, and which decisions were made by the system
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Every reported investigation has a complete row, with zero human actions left unclassified as decision-making or non-decision-making
success_check.evidence_needed: The published boundary table and the run logs used to populate it
